vehicles.name: Carro Armato M13/40
vehicles.notes: Italy's main tank in North Africa. Outclassed by British Matilda and Crusader tanks; suffered from poor crew conditions and mechanical unreliability in desert conditions.

vehicles.name: Carro Armato P26/40
vehicles.notes: Italy's most powerful tank design. Production was delayed and only ~100 were completed, mostly after Italy's armistice; used by German forces in Italy.

vehicles.name: R-2 (LT vz. 35)
vehicles.notes: Czech-designed tank purchased by Romania. Used on the Eastern Front in 1941. Outclassed by Soviet T-34s by 1942.

vehicles.name: Toldi II
vehicles.notes: Hungary's standard light tank on the Eastern Front. Too lightly armoured and armed to fight Soviet armour effectively; used for reconnaissance and infantry support.
